content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: The puzzle is unique, fun and just challenging enough to be easy enough for beginners to solve, yet still be competitive for intermediate coders.

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: This is the kind of clash I like, easy to understand, the example is self-explanatory, I saw you've already had quite a few relevant reviews (I wanted to specify the case of N=26 but I saw the problem you had so it's not a big deal)
For me, it belongs in a Clash of Code 😁

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: This is a really great contribution. It was kinda easy, but Clash of Code shouldn't be too hard anyway. This puzzle actually had some critical thinking involved with manipulating string values and lengths, and I think that's why this puzzle deserves to be part of Clash of Code. Nice job!

Hey! Some feedback from me:
Goal
Please reformat N to [[N]] (variable)
Please reformat i to [[i]] (variable)
Please reformat the constants {{A}}, {{B}}, and {{C}}.

Input
Please reformat N to [[N]].

Output
Please reformat N to [[N]].
Please reformat i to [[i]]

Suggestion: Maybe don't tell them how to solve it, just set the statement to a double pyramid?

Constraints
Please reformat to [[N]].
Please reformat constants: {{1}}, {{26}}

Testcases
Your constraint states that N can go up to 26. Maybe include a testcase with N=26 or a higher number like that?

Stub generator:
instead of write print the pyramids, maybe use a loop to write multiple lines since that is the required output format.

Good contribution though.

All suggestions from @Vincent18 are good, so please make sure to follow them. Here are my some additional suggestions from my side.

Formatting and presentation
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format your statement properly as suggested by @Vincent18.

* Use the example on that page as a reference for both formatting and presentation of different sections of the statement.

Goal
* Please change:

For each row i (starting from 1), print the i-th uppercase letter of the alphabet repeated i times.
Each row must be printed twice, forming two identical pyramids side-by-side separated by spaces.

to:

For each row [[i]] (starting from {{1}}), print the [[i]]-th uppercase letter of the alphabet repeated [[i]] times on both the left and the right, with spaces between them so that the total width of the row is {{2 x }}[[N]].

Output
* Please delete everything currently in the section and replace it with:

<<[[N]] lines>>: The double alphabet pyramid pattern of [[N]] rows.

Cases
* Validator 4: Please remove the trailing newline from the output.

* Please add at least two more pairs of cases (i.e. 4 cases in total):
  • One pair: [[N]] is around 13.
  • One pair: [[N]] is 25 in one case and 26 in the other.

Thanks.

Hey! Thanks for the helpful feedback. 

I've updated the contribution and reformatted the variables ([[N]], [[i]]) and constants ({{A}}, {{1}}, {{26}}) as suggested. I also simplified the statement and adjusted the stub generator.

Regarding the testcase with N = 26: I attempted to add it, but the output becomes extremely large, and the editor makes it difficult to manage such a long expected output. Because of that, I kept the current testcases but ensured the constraints still allow up to [[N]] = {{26}}.

If you think it's important, I can try adding it again in a different way or reducing the spacing so the output is shorter. Thanks again for reviewing the contribution!

Thank you for updating the contribution. However, the statement now appears to be over-modified, and some important details have been removed. You're also correct in pointing out that the output has a maximum character limit (it's 1000). Please see my updated comments below.

Goal
* Please add this to the end:

For each row [[i]] from {{1}} to [[N]], print the [[i]]-th uppercase letter of the alphabet repeated [[i]] times on both sides, with spaces between them so that the total width of the row is {{2 x }}[[N]].

For example, the first row starts with {{A}}, the second row with {{B}}, the third with {{C}}, and so on.

Output
* Note that the Output section should contain a simple description only. Please delete everything currently in this section and replace it with:

<<[[N]] lines>>: The double alphabet pyramid pattern of [[N]] rows.

Cases
* Validator 4: Please remove the trailing newline from the output.

* Please add at least two more pairs of cases (i.e. 4 cases in total):
  • One pair: [[N]] is around 13.
  • One pair: [[N]] is 21 in one case and 22 in the other.

Thanks again.

Hi! Thanks for the detailed feedback. I have implemented the changes you suggested:

* Added the additional explanation in the **Goal** section with the correct formatting for [[i]], [[N]], and the constants {{A}}, {{B}}, {{C}}, etc.

* Updated the **Output** section to the simplified description.

* Removed the trailing newline from **Validator 4**.

* Added the additional test cases where [[N]] is around 13 and also where [[N]] is 21 and 22.

However, the maximum value of [[N]] for which the output box currently accepts the full expected output is  21. For larger values like 22 or 26 the output becomes too large for the field to handle.

Please let me know if there is a recommended workaround for larger outputs. Thanks again for reviewing the contribution!

Thank you for the quick update.

I thought 22 would still be acceptable. With newlines included, the total number of characters in the output should be around 990. That said, 20 is perfectly fine, so there's no need to update the cases again.

One possible way to handle very long outputs would be to require the player to output only the first 1000 characters. However, I don't think it's worth the extra effort to implement that for this problem.

Please let me know if you have any other questions!
Thanks for the clarification! I appreciate the suggestion and the detailed feedback throughout the review process.

Hey!
Just wanted to know, what's the procedure for getting the contribution approved?

Your clash of code will be reviewed by other reviewers. If they provide feedback, you can improve the contribution based on their comments. Once three reviewers are satisfied and click the APPROVE button above, it'll be approved.
